message:
feat(fcp-testkit): complete live-suite infrastructure and runtime lifecycle acceptance tests

Close bead 49z0b.14.2: Add BudgetAlert threshold system (ok/warning/critical/exceeded),
StaleResourceReport for orphaned synthetic test resource scanning, PrerequisiteReport
for structured validation of all live-suite prerequisites, manifest JSON serialization
and file loading, and 88 unit tests for live_suite.rs.

Add runtime_lifecycle_acceptance.rs integration test file (30 tests) covering health
tracker state machine, supervisor config validation, polling cursor lifecycle, streaming
session heartbeat tracking, session-script DSL, evidence collection, cleanup guards with
synthetic tenant teardown, and HealthState serialization contracts.

Fix workspace formatting drift across 6 connectors via cargo fmt.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.14","title":"Provider-sandbox and nightly live-suite program for connectors that cannot stop at local fixtures","description":"Background:
- Some connectors cannot be fully proven by local fixtures alone; they need vendor sandboxes, bridge daemons, or nightly live runs with controlled credentials and cost caps.
- Without a formal live-suite policy, teams either skip live checks entirely or run fragile ad hoc scripts that produce poor diagnostics.

Deliverables:
- Define and implement the provider-sandbox/nightly-live layer for connectors that need it.
- Establish secret management, account provisioning, cost ceilings, cleanup policies, scheduling, flaky-test quarantine, and artifact retention.
- Record which connectors must have live suites, which can rely on local fixtures, and which require both.

Acceptance criteria:
- Live verification is part of the official testing story rather than tribal knowledge.
- Failures from nightly/provider suites produce the same structured artifacts and redaction guarantees as local E2E runs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:38.290317Z","created_by":"LilacLake","updated_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:48.970913Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","live","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.14","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:38.290317Z","created_by":"LilacLake","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.14","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:47.980156Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.14","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:48.229073Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.14","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:48.477071Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.14","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:48.721570Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.14","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:48.970319Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.15","title":"CI gates, coverage dashboards, and maintainer playbooks for the testing-completeness program","description":"Background:
- A testing program is not real until CI and maintainer workflows enforce it.
- The workspace needs both a quality gate and a practical operator story for running, triaging, and extending the new suites.

Deliverables:
- Add CI/reporting gates that fail when connectors lack required suite classification, when E2E artifacts do not validate, or when redaction/logging policies are violated.
- Generate machine-readable and human-readable dashboards for coverage status by connector family and archetype.
- Publish maintainer/operator playbooks describing how to run local acceptance suites, how nightly live suites behave, how to inspect artifacts, and how to classify or quarantine flakes.
- Ensure new connector work cannot silently ship with only fake-backed tests unless explicitly exempted under the policy.

Acceptance criteria:
- Coverage policy and artifact policy are enforceable by automation.
- A future agent can pick up any connector and know exactly which suites to run and how to debug Deliverables:
- Expand no-mock integration and host-backed coverage for lifecycle, policy, receipts, cancellation, restart/recovery, rollout, telemetry, redaction, and schema/logging guarantees.
- Close gaps where current coverage stops at golden vectors or mock-backed helpers.
- Make core-crate failures easy to localize via structured artifacts rather than giant opaque test logs.

+## Standard Live-Suite Prerequisites
+
+Bead `49z0b.14.2` standardizes the per-connector prerequisite declaration
+through `fcp_testkit::live_suite::EnvironmentManifest`. Connectors in Tier
+B/C/D/E should use that layer instead of ad hoc env-var checks or one-off
+cleanup notes.
+
+### Required Manifest Fields
+
+Every live-capable connector should declare all of the following:
+
+- connector id and provider name
+- tier gate: `sandbox`, `device`, `read_only`, or `live_write`
+- required secrets and any required non-secret environment variables
+- explicit account setup guidance for the sandbox, test tenant, device lab, or
+  dedicated workspace required to run safely
+- per-run budget ceiling in USD
+- cleanup strategy
+- rate-limit guidance when the provider enforces quotas or anti-abuse delays
+- optional metadata needed by evidence collectors or nightly orchestration
+
+Use the constructor that matches the classification table:
+
+- Tier A: `EnvironmentManifest::local(...)`
+- Tier B: `EnvironmentManifest::sandbox(...)`
+- Tier C: `EnvironmentManifest::device(...)`
+- Tier D: `EnvironmentManifest::read_only(...)`
+- Tier E: `EnvironmentManifest::live_write(...)`
+
+### Gate Mapping
+
+| Tier | Gate | Expected Secret Boundary |
+| --- | --- | --- |
+| Tier B `sandbox_required` | `FCP_LIVE_SANDBOX=1` | provider sandbox / test account |
+| Tier C `device_required` | `FCP_LIVE_DEVICE=1` | dedicated device, lab host, or platform account |
+| Tier D `live_read_only` | `FCP_LIVE_READ=1` | read-only or low-risk API key |
+| Tier E `live_write_required` | `FCP_LIVE_WRITE=1` | isolated mutation-capable test tenant |
+
+### Safety Rules For Repeated Automated Use
+
+- Never point these suites at production credentials, production tenants, or a
+  shared personal account.
+- Namespace mutable resources with a synthetic tenant or other run-specific
+  prefix so later cleanup and forensic inspection stay deterministic.
+- Tier B and Tier E suites must declare a cleanup strategy. Tier C suites may
+  use `CleanupStrategy::None` only when the device/local platform boundary does
+  not create cloud-side mutable state that the suite itself is responsible for
+  removing.
+- Rate-limit metadata should be truthful enough for nightly orchestration to
+  slow down or quarantine a connector rather than burn quota blindly.
+- Missing prerequisites should fail as an explicit gated skip with actionable
+  remediation, not as a mysterious auth or network error mid-run.
+
+### Evidence Bundle Expectations
+
+For Tier B/C/D/E runs, `environment.json` or the connector-local equivalent
+should preserve enough redaction-safe context to explain why a run was safe and
+repeatable:
+
+- suite class and live tier
+- redacted secret/env-var presence, not raw values
+- account setup or sandbox identity in redacted form
+- budget ceiling and observed spend summary
+- cleanup expectations and whether mutation was exercised
+- synthetic tenant identity or equivalent run namespace
+- rate-limit guidance used during the run
+
